events.title: 🌷 AI Workshop for Content Creators (Spring 2026)
events.tagline: Get real results from your content with AI-powered workflows.

speakers.name: Bill Raymond
speakers.title: Host
speakers.shortBio: Bill is a technical content strategist and AI practitioner who uses generative tools daily to ship human-centered documentation and learning experiences.

faq.question: Do I need prior AI experience to join?

blocks.text: No prior AI experience is required. This training is designed for beginners and is still useful for experienced participants who want a more reliable workflow.

faq.question: Do I need anything before the workshop starts?

blocks.text: By the time you take this course, you must have a paid AI account from ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, or Microsoft 365 Copilot.

faq.question: How is the workshop delivered, and what if I miss a session?

blocks.text: Sessions are hosted live on Zoom. If you miss a session, there are no official makeup dates, but Bill accommodates reasonable scheduling-conflict requests when possible and shares the best available catch-up option.

faq.question: What will I get by the end (certificate, exercises, and take-home work)?

blocks.text: You will complete hands-on exercises, receive practical take-home materials, and earn a digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.

faq.question: How can I ask questions before enrolling, and what if I can’t make this cohort?

blocks.text: If you have questions before enrolling, use the Cambermast contact form. If this cohort timing does not work, we can share upcoming options on the events calendar.

faq.question: 📧 Do you have a manager budget-approval letter I can send?

blocks.text: Yes. Use the email template below, open it in your email client, or copy it with one click.

blocks.subject: Request for approval: AI training program
blocks.body:
Hi [Manager Name],

I would like approval to attend [Program Name] with Cambermast.

This training is focused on practical, responsible AI use and includes
hands-on exercises I can apply directly in my role. I expect this will
help me improve speed and quality in [team/process area], while following
our policies and quality standards.

Program: [Program Name]
Dates: [Dates]
Cost: [Cost]
Link: [Program/Event URL]

After the training, I will share key takeaways and specific ways we can
apply what I learned to our team.

Thank you for considering this request.

Best,
[Your Name]

description.summary: Across weekly 2-hour sessions, participants combine strategy discussions with hands-on practice to automate repetitive tasks, preserve brand voice, and publish higher-impact content faster.
description.bodyMd:
The AI Workshop for Tech Writers and Content Creators is a live, seven-week cohort that helps technical communicators and content teams weave generative AI into their daily production workflows.

## Outcomes
- Automate repetitive content production tasks and accelerate delivery timelines.
- Tailor generative AI outputs to match established voice, tone, and terminology.
- Connect content decisions to audience journeys, measurable outcomes, and ROI.

## What You Will Build
- Persona-driven workflows that use AI tools to surface gaps and opportunities.
- Ready-to-use prompt libraries, custom GPT instructions, and evaluation checklists.
- A documented project plan that operationalizes AI-assisted content production.
- Digital certificate of completion once the Week 7 project is presented.

## Who This Is For
- Technical communication and documentation specialists modernizing their workflows with AI.
- Content strategists responsible for aligning deliverables to user journeys and business goals.
- Knowledge teams supporting structured and unstructured content across the enterprise.

highlights: Automate repetitive content production tasks with AI
Preserve brand voice with reusable prompts and guardrails
Ship a final project and earn a completion certificate
audienceBullets: Technical communication and documentation specialists modernizing their workflows with AI.
Content strategists responsible for aligning deliverables to user journeys and business goals.
Knowledge teams supporting structured and unstructured content across the enterprise.
buildBullets: Persona-driven workflows that use AI tools to surface gaps and opportunities.
Ready-to-use prompt libraries, custom GPT instructions, and evaluation checklists.
A documented project plan that operationalizes AI-assisted content production.
Digital certificate of completion once the Week 7 project is presented.
outcomes: Automate repetitive content production tasks and accelerate delivery timelines.
Tailor generative AI outputs to match established voice, tone, and terminology.
Connect content decisions to audience journeys, measurable outcomes, and ROI.

agenda.title: Week 1: Adopting the AI Mindset
agenda.outcome: Explore AI foundations for content teams and align on ethical guardrails.
agenda.details: Map quick wins that remove repetitive production work. Practice prompt basics for clarity, structure, and factual rigor.

agenda.title: Week 2: Mapping Your Audience Journey
agenda.outcome: Build personas and user journeys that guide AI-augmented content.
agenda.details: Align messaging and assets to each audience touchpoint. Test AI tools that simulate reader questions and expectations.

agenda.title: Week 3: Personalization at Scale
agenda.outcome: Deliver platform-specific versions without sacrificing consistency.
agenda.details: Automate tone, style, and format adjustments with controlled prompts. Design custom GPT instructions to replicate your brand voice.

agenda.title: Week 4: Accelerating Value Delivery
agenda.outcome: Streamline production workflows and remove bottlenecks.
agenda.details: Run AI-powered content audits to prioritize improvements. Plan projects and tasks that increase overall content ROI.

agenda.title: Week 5: Deep Content Insights
agenda.outcome: Identify gaps, new opportunities, and research angles with AI.
agenda.details: Develop outlines and drafts for net-new assets. Define the project you will evolve through Week 7.

agenda.title: Week 6: Data-Driven Prioritization
agenda.outcome: Use customer insights to prioritize content that converts.
agenda.details: Apply advanced prompting techniques to sharpen clarity. Review progress on your individual or team project.

agenda.title: Week 7: Evolving with AI
agenda.outcome: Present final projects and gather peer plus mentor feedback.
agenda.details: Codify a long-term roadmap for AI-enabled content operations. Operationalize your workflow and celebrate the cohort completion.

events.title: 💻 Ship Your First App with AI (April 2026)

faq.question: Do I need prior AI experience to join?

blocks.text: Yes, some prior AI experience is recommended for this workshop. It is designed for builders, operators, consultants, IT professionals, and aspiring developers who already want to use AI to build and improve practical web applications.

faq.question: What do I need before the workshop starts?

blocks.text: Complete the full setup guide before Week 1. You should have Visual Studio Code, a Codex or Claude extension, Git, GitHub, Docker Desktop, and a paid Claude or ChatGPT account ready to use. You should also have a laptop, stable internet, and a project idea or workflow you want to turn into a practical web app.

blocks.label: Open the Ship Your First App with AI setup guide

faq.question: How is the workshop delivered, and what if I miss a session?

blocks.text: Cohorts are delivered live using Zoom, Google Meet, Microsoft Teams, or another proven collaboration platform. If you miss a session, contact us and we will share the best available catch-up option.

faq.question: What will I get by the end (certificate, exercises, and take-home work)?

blocks.text: You will complete hands-on exercises and leave with practical take-home materials. Certificate availability may vary by program or event and is listed in the event details.
Yes. Every participant receives at least one hour of free consultation after the training, plus any additional support listed for that specific program or event.

faq.question: How can I ask questions before enrolling, and what if I can’t make this cohort?

blocks.text: If you have questions before enrolling, reach out any time. If this cohort timing does not work, we can share upcoming options.

faq.question: 📧 Do you have a manager budget-approval letter I can send?

blocks.text: Yes. This version is tailored to Ship Your First App with AI and auto-fills the course title, dates, cost, and link from the event details below.

blocks.subject: Request for approval: [Program Name]
blocks.body:
Hi [Manager Name],

I would like approval to attend [Program Name] with Cambermast.

This training is focused on building and improving a practical web application with AI. It covers hands-on app development, Git, GitHub, Docker, AI planning features, and a capstone project I can apply directly to my role.

Program: [Program Name]
Dates: [Dates]
Cost: [Cost]
Link: [Program/Event URL]

I expect this training will help me improve speed, quality, and technical confidence while giving me a more structured workflow for building useful internal or client-facing tools.

Thank you for considering this request.

Best,
[Your Name]

faq.question: Do I need prior coding experience to join?

blocks.text: No formal developer background is required. This workshop is designed for semi-technical participants, including IT professionals, aspiring developers, and people who want guided practice building a practical web app.

faq.question: Is this workshop tied to one coding product or platform?

blocks.text: No. The workshop teaches transferable skills and workflows that can be applied across AI coding tools, chat interfaces, editor integrations, and future platforms.

faq.question: What kind of web app can I build during the cohort?

blocks.text: Most participants build a practical web app or internal tool tied to a real workflow they already care about. That could include an operations dashboard, intake form, workflow helper, internal utility, or another lightweight product you want to keep improving after the workshop.

faq.question: Will we cover Git, GitHub, and Docker?

blocks.text: Yes. Week 2 introduces Git and GitHub for version control and collaboration, plus Docker for creating a consistent development environment. The goal is practical familiarity so you can keep improving your app with a more professional workflow.

faq.question: How does the capstone project work?

blocks.text: You will shape your capstone as the workshop progresses, using the exercises and weekly feedback to improve it over time. In Week 4, you finalize the project, present what you built, and leave with clear next steps for continuing development after the cohort ends.

faq.question: Will we use AI planning features to make the app more robust?

blocks.text: Yes. In Week 3, the workshop focuses on using AI planning features to break larger goals into manageable steps, improve the structure of your app, and make the product easier to extend, test, and maintain.

description.summary: Across weekly 2-hour sessions, participants learn how to turn plain-language ideas into a working web app, improve it with modern development workflows, and strengthen it with AI-supported planning and iteration. You will work through guided exercises, apply what you learn to a real use case, and leave with a repeatable workflow you can use long after the cohort ends.
description.bodyMd:
Ship Your First App with AI is a live, four-week hands-on coding cohort that helps semi-technical professionals use AI to plan, build, and improve a practical web application.

## Outcomes
- Understand how AI-assisted coding works and where it fits into real-world workflows.
- Turn plain-language requests into clear technical instructions that lead to usable code.
- Build practical web applications, tools, and automations with guided support.
- Improve AI-generated code through testing, debugging, refactoring, and review.
- Apply practical guardrails that make AI-assisted coding more reliable and maintainable.

## What You Will Build
- A repeatable workflow for planning, building, testing, and improving code with AI.
- Prompt patterns and review habits that help you get better coding results.
- A practical web app or internal tool you can continue improving after the workshop.
- Greater confidence using AI to support technical work without relying on one platform.
- A digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.

## Prerequisites
- Review the FAQ item "What do I need before the workshop starts?" and complete that setup before Week 1.
- Visual Studio Code installed on your laptop.
- Git installed on your laptop.
- Docker installed and ready to run locally.
- A GitHub account you can use during the workshop.
- A paid Claude or ChatGPT account you can use during the workshop.
- Comfort using a laptop, browser, and basic technical tools.
- A real workflow, problem, or project idea you want to explore during the cohort.

## Who This Is For
- Builders who want to move faster with AI-assisted coding workflows.
- Operators looking to create internal tools, web apps, and automations.
- Consultants who want to prototype solutions for client and internal work.
- IT professionals exploring practical ways to use AI for coding and technical problem solving.
- Aspiring developers who want guided practice building with AI.

highlights: Turn plain-language ideas into a working web app
Practice using Git, GitHub, Docker, and AI-assisted iteration
Ship a capstone project and earn a completion certificate
audienceBullets: Builders who want to move faster with AI-assisted coding workflows.
Operators looking to create internal tools, web apps, and automations.
Consultants who want to prototype solutions for client and internal work.
IT professionals exploring practical ways to use AI for coding and technical problem solving.
Aspiring developers who want guided practice building with AI.
buildBullets: A repeatable workflow for planning, building, testing, and improving code with AI.
Prompt patterns and review habits that help you get better coding results.
A practical web app or internal tool you can continue improving after the workshop.
Greater confidence using AI to support technical work without relying on one platform.
A digital certificate of completion after presenting your final project.
outcomes: Understand how AI-assisted coding works and where it fits into real-world workflows.
Turn plain-language requests into clear technical instructions that lead to usable code.
Build practical web applications, tools, and automations with guided support.
Improve AI-generated code through testing, debugging, refactoring, and review.
Apply practical guardrails that make AI-assisted coding more reliable and maintainable.

agenda.title: Week 1: Build Your First Web App
agenda.outcome: Create a working web app and understand how AI-assisted coding fits into practical development workflows.
agenda.details: Define what AI-assisted coding is and where it works well. Compare common AI coding workflows across chat and editor-based tools. Learn how to describe coding tasks clearly using plain language. Create a working web app and review the structure, readability, and flow of the generated code.

agenda.title: Week 2: Git, GitHub, Docker, and Iteration
agenda.outcome: Set up a more professional workflow for improving the app you started in Week 1.
agenda.details: Learn the basics of Git and GitHub for version control and collaboration. Use Docker to create a consistent development environment for your app. Practice revising requests when the first output is incomplete or off track. Improve the web app you started in Week 1 through guided iteration.

agenda.title: Week 3: Planning for a More Robust Product
agenda.outcome: Use AI planning features to make your app more robust and easier to scale.
agenda.details: Use AI planning features to break larger product goals into manageable development steps. Build a more robust web app with clearer structure and room to scale. Add validation, edge-case handling, and simple testing habits. Document assumptions, dependencies, and next steps.

agenda.title: Week 4: Capstone Project and Next Steps
agenda.outcome: Create and present a capstone project built during the cohort.
agenda.details: Create and finalize a capstone project built during the cohort. Present your project and receive feedback. Review how to evaluate code quality, maintainability, and fit for use. Leave with a roadmap for continuing your AI-assisted coding practice.
